paper_title: Morphological and Functional Analysis of Santacrucian Sloths

methods.description: Collection and preparation of fossil and extant mammal specimens for morphometric analysis.

steps.description: Specimens used in this study are listed in Appendix I. The fossil specimens studied include those collected by expeditions carried out during the nineteenth and early twentieth centuries and material collected by MLP-Duke University joint expeditions over the last eight years.
steps.searchable_summary: collect fossil specimens from various expeditions

steps.description: Twenty-one linear measurements were defined and taken on bones of the forelimb and pectoral girdle.
steps.searchable_summary: take linear measurements on forelimb bones

steps.description: Some measurements were unattainable, especially among the fossil specimens, and these were estimated from global mean values for the Principal Component Analysis (PCA).
steps.searchable_summary: estimate missing measurements using global mean values

methods.description: Perform PCA on forelimb measurements to explore shape variation among specimens.

steps.description: A PCA was performed on all 21 forelimb measurements of the 15 most complete specimens of Santacrucian sloths and all included extant mammals.
Base-ten logarithms of the raw measurements were used to perform the analysis.

methods.description: Calculate functional indices to distinguish taxa by substrate preference or use.

steps.description: Calculate the Brachial Index (BI), Index of Fossorial Ability (IFA), and Distal Epiphyseal Index (DEI) using specific formulas.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate BI, IFA, and DEI indices

steps.description: Functional indices were plotted in boxplots, showing the same data in two different ways: index values for extant mammals were plotted using alternately substrate preference categories and substrate-use categories regarding digging activity.
